title: China’s New AI Is 6X More Efficient Than Claude
summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-weight model designed for coding agents working on real codebases. Z.ai simultaneously released GLM-5.2, another open model with a 1 million token context window for long-horizon software tasks, both demonstrating efficiency gains and competitive performance against proprietary systems.
writeup: The video covers two open-weight model releases aimed at coding work: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.7 Code, built for coding agents operating on real codebases, and Z.ai's GLM-5.2, which carries a 1 million token context window for long-horizon software tasks. Both are positioned as efficiency gains that compete with proprietary systems. The takeaway is that open-weight Chinese models are targeting agentic code generation directly against closed alternatives, with extended context and lower compute cost as the differentiators.
